units_list.unit_name: Unit 06 — Introduction to Digital Graphics and Animation
Unit 01 — IT Systems: Strategy, Management and Infrastructure
units_list.unit_name_ar: نظم تكنولوجيا المعلومات - الإستراتيجية والإدارة والبنية التحتية

units_list.unit_name: Unit 12 — IT Technical Support and Management
Unit 11 — Cyber Security and Incident Management

L2_1.unit_name: Using IT for Information and Communications in Organisations

learning_aims.A: Understand how IT supports information and communication in organisations.
learning_aims.B: Use IT tools to collect, process and present information for a given purpose.

criteria.description: Explain how IT is used for information and communication in an organisation.
Describe types of information and appropriate IT tools.
Use IT tools to produce information outputs that meet a brief.
Review how well IT outputs meet audience and purpose.

learning_aims.A: Understand data modelling and spreadsheet concepts.
learning_aims.B: Develop spreadsheet and data models for a scenario.

criteria.description: Explain entities, fields, relationships and spreadsheet features.
Describe validation, formulas and functions used in models.
Build a working spreadsheet/data model with calculations.
Improve accuracy, structure or presentation of the model.

learning_aims.A: Understand network hardware, topologies and protocols at introductory level.
learning_aims.B: Document or configure a simple network (simulated or physical).

criteria.description: Describe common network devices, media and topologies.
Explain basic addressing and connectivity concepts.
Produce a simple network design or configuration with evidence.
Explain troubleshooting steps for common connectivity issues.

learning_aims.A: Understand variables, control flow, input/output and testing.
learning_aims.B: Implement a short program that solves a given problem.

criteria.description: Outline algorithm steps and programming constructs for the problem.
Describe testing approaches used.
Implement code that runs and meets basic requirements.
Refactor or extend code for clarity or error handling.

L2_6.unit_name: Introduction to Digital Graphics and Animation

learning_aims.A: Understand bitmap/vector graphics and animation techniques.
learning_aims.B: Create digital graphics and/or a short animation from a brief.

criteria.description: Compare graphics formats and animation methods for a use case.
Produce graphics or animation assets that follow a visual brief.
Justify design choices and refine using feedback.

learning_aims.A: Understand web technologies, structure and accessibility basics.
learning_aims.B: Design and build a simple multi-page website.

criteria.description: Explain HTML/CSS structure, navigation and hosting basics.
Build pages that meet layout and content requirements.
Improve usability, responsiveness or accessibility.

learning_aims.A: Understand application design, UI and event-driven concepts.
learning_aims.B: Build a simple interactive application or prototype.

criteria.description: Describe navigation, usability and core app components.
Implement interactions that meet a simple brief.
Test and fix issues affecting usability.

learning_aims.A: Understand game genres, mechanics, assets and audience.
learning_aims.B: Design and produce a simple game or playable prototype.

criteria.description: Explain core mechanics, assets and target audience for the game idea.
Produce a playable prototype or design with evidence.
Improve the design using playtesting or peer feedback.

L3_1.unit_name: IT Systems: Strategy, Management and Infrastructure

learning_aims.A: Understand the value of information and IT systems in organisations.
learning_aims.B: Understand how organisations use IT strategy, management and infrastructure.

criteria.description: Explain the characteristics of good information.
Explain issues related to the use of information.
Analyse the impact of different information types.
Explain how IT enables value for organisations.

learning_aims.B: Design, code, and test a solution.

criteria.description: Explain language constructs used in the solution.
Implement code that meets functional requirements.
Improve reliability or structure with testing evidence.

learning_aims.B: Design and build a multi-page site.

criteria.description: Explain structure, accessibility, and hosting basics.
Build pages that meet layout and content requirements.
Enhance UX or responsiveness.

learning_aims.A: Understand game assets, mechanics and audience.
learning_aims.B: Design and implement a playable game or prototype.

criteria.description: Explain core game mechanics, assets, tools and audience for the chosen genre.
Implement gameplay features and assets aligned to the design specification.
Iteratively improve the product using testing evidence.

learning_aims.A: Understand the roles and phases of IT project management.
learning_aims.B: Plan, monitor and review an IT project with appropriate documentation.

criteria.description: Explain key project phases, resources, risks and stakeholder communication.
Produce project plans, milestones and progress records with evidence.
Evaluate project outcomes against objectives with lessons learned.

learning_aims.A: Understand threats, vulnerabilities and defensive controls.
learning_aims.B: Apply security procedures and respond to incidents.

criteria.description: Explain common threats, vulnerabilities and mitigation techniques.
Document security controls and incident handling steps with evidence.
Assess effectiveness of controls against a scenario.

learning_aims.A: Understand support processes and service level requirements.
learning_aims.B: Deliver diagnostic and resolution support using appropriate tools.

criteria.description: Explain ticketing, escalation and user communication in IT support.
Diagnose and document fixes with reproducible steps and logs/screenshots.
Evaluate support performance against agreed metrics.

learning_aims.A: Understand AI concepts, data and ethics.
learning_aims.B: Apply a simple AI/ML workflow to a problem.

criteria.description: Explain supervised/unsupervised learning, data quality and bias at an appropriate level.
Train or use a model/toolchain and report results with evidence.
Evaluate limitations, risks and ethical implications of the solution.
